rating.coverage_remarks: Covers core concepts (decidability, halting problem) but lacks completion of halting problem proof and deeper exploration of Rice's Theorem/reduction techniques. Recursively enumerable languages mentioned but not contrasted sufficiently with decidable ones.
rating.quality_remarks: Generally accurate but contains incomplete sections (cut-off halting problem proof, truncated MCQ explanation). Flashcard and first MCQ are well-constructed but need more numerical/application-based questions.
rating.tier_remarks: Appropriate formal language but needs more rigorous proofs (e.g., complete diagonalization argument) and advanced applications for graduate-level students. Current depth leans introductory rather than thorough.
suggestions: Complete halting problem proof with full diagonalization/self-reference explanation
Add concrete examples of undecidable problems using Rice's Theorem and reduction methods
Include numerical MCQs (e.g., reductions between problems) and contrast recursively enumerable vs decidable languages with formal definitions
